Investment Memorandum · Residential Fix-and-Flip

Alvorada 4, Arabian Ranches 1

3-to-4 Bedroom Villa · Emaar · Acquire · Renovate · Extend · Resell
Palm & PeakJuly 2026 · AED · All-equity

Executive summary

Palm & Peak proposes to acquire, renovate and resell a 3-bedroom villa in Alvorada 4, Arabian Ranches 1, an established low-density Emaar community with deep end-user demand. The villa is acquired at AED 9.0M, upgraded through a AED 2.0M full renovation that reconfigures the layout from three bedrooms to four and adds a 300 sqft permitted extension, and resold at a target of AED 16.0M within a 6 to 12 month window.

Equity required
all-in

The exit is evidenced, not assumed. An identical Alvorada 4 / 3-bedroom villa on the same 8,080 sqft plot sold for AED 8.8M in September 2025 and resold, renovated, at exactly AED 16.0M in April 2026, a registered Dubai Land Department transaction. Twenty Alvorada villa sales cleared in the past twelve months, confirming a liquid, repeatable market.

Investment thesis

  • Proven exit. A same-footprint renovated Alvorada 4 villa printed AED 16.0M three months ago (DLD registered).
  • Liquid market. 20 registered Alvorada villa sales in 12 months; well-priced stock in Arabian Ranches typically absorbs in 4 to 8 weeks.
  • Controlled value-add. Full interior refit plus a modest, permitted 300 sqft extension at Emaar's tier-1 FAR fee.
  • Downside cushion. Unrenovated same-footprint stock trades AED 8.8M to 9.1M, close to our entry.
  • Clean structure. All-equity, no financing or refinancing risk; single asset, single exit.

Business plan

1

Acquire

Purchase at AED 9.0M. Plot 8,291 sqft, BUA 3,778 sqft, 3-bedroom. Transfer registered through the Dubai Land Department under an all-equity structure.

2

Renovate & extend

AED 2.0M full interior and exterior refit. Reconfigure the layout to convert the 3-bedroom into a 4-bedroom, and add a permitted 300 sqft extension (study over the entrance, en-suite upgrades). Circa 7 months of works.

3

Resell

List at a target AED 16.0M, roughly 3,924 AED/sqft on post-extension BUA, in line with the renovated comparable set. Typical absorption 4 to 8 weeks at market price.

Profit distribution  ·  Al Mulla JV waterfall

The investor funds 100% of the capital. Profit is split on the project's annualised IRR, stepping the operator's share up as returns rise. This mirrors the Palm & Peak / Al Mulla collaboration agreement in place on Sunrise Bay Tower 2.

Sensitivity  ·  exit price and time to sell

Proof of exit  ·  the identical villa round-trip

The clearest evidence for the 16.0M target is a same-footprint Alvorada 4 / 3-bedroom villa on the same 8,080 sqft plot (3,767 BUA) that completed this exact acquire-renovate-resell cycle in the last twelve months. Both legs are registered with the Dubai Land Department.

A second data point: a renovated Alvorada 3 / 3-bed villa sold for AED 15,800,000 (4,194 AED/sqft on BUA) in November 2025, both highlighted in the table below.

Source: DXB Interact / Dubai Land Department, registered transactions. AED/sqft computed on BUA. Highlighted rows: the twin Alvorada 4 round-trip (8.80M Sep-2025, resold 16.00M Apr-2026) and a renovated Alvorada 3 that sold 15.80M at 4,194 AED/sqft.

Key risks and mitigants

RiskMitigant
Exit price sits at the top of the community rangeAnchored to a same-footprint renovated comparable that printed 16.0M in April 2026. Sensitivity shows positive returns down to a 14.0M exit.
Renovation cost overrunFixed-scope budget with staged contractor draws and a held contingency; scope defined before acquisition.
Time to sell extendsHolding cost is modest (circa AED 32k per year). IRR remains strong even at a 12-month hold; the grid above quantifies the effect.
Broader market softeningEntry near the unrenovated floor (8.8M to 9.1M) provides a cushion; Arabian Ranches is end-user driven and less cyclical than investor-led districts.
Extension approvalExtension sized within Emaar's standard tier-1 FAR allowance; the base case does not depend on approvals beyond the modest 300 sqft.

Indicative timeline

Month 0: acquire and register transfer. Months 1 to 7: renovation and permitted extension. Months 7 to 9: list, market and secure buyer. Full round-trip targeted within 6 to 12 months, consistent with the September 2025 to April 2026 twin-comp cycle.

Palm & Peak · Alvorada 4 fix-and-flip investment memorandum · prepared July 2026 · all figures AED, all-equity basis.
Illustrative underwriting based on registered Dubai Land Department comparables. This document is not an offer, a solicitation, or a guarantee of return, and is provided for discussion only.
